106	pub struct AnswerBlob(pub String);
107	// SECTION: WebRtcTransport (impl Transport)
108	
109	/// `Transport` impl backed by a single `RtcDataChannel`. Shared by both
110	/// joiner and host sides — the same DataChannel just carries different
111	/// payloads in each direction (joiner sends `ClientMsg`, receives
112	/// `ServerMsg`; host the inverse, but Phase 4 wraps it differently
113	/// rather than reusing this trait directly for the host send path).
114	pub struct WebRtcTransport {
115	    /// `Rc<RefCell<...>>` so the DataChannel reference can be late-bound
116	    /// (joiner side discovers it via `ondatachannel` when the host's
117	    /// channel arrives, after `connect_as_joiner` returns).
118	    dc: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>>,
119	}
120	
121	impl Transport for WebRtcTransport {
122	    fn send(&self, msg: ClientMsg) -> bool {
123	        let dc = self.dc.borrow();
124	        let dc = match dc.as_ref() {
125	            Some(dc) => dc,
126	            None => return false,
127	        };
128	        if dc.ready_state() != RtcDataChannelState::Open {
129	            return false;
130	        }
131	        let body = match serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
132	            Ok(s) => s,
133	            Err(_) => return false,
134	        };
135	        dc.send_with_str(&body).is_ok()
136	    }
137	}
138	// SECTION: HostHandshake
139	
140	/// What `connect_as_host` returns.
141	///
142	/// Phase 4 (`host_room.rs`) consumes this: it owns the `Room` state
143	/// machine, holds the `pc` + `dc` references, and routes incoming
144	/// `ClientMsg`s through `Room::apply` to produce per-peer `ServerMsg`
145	/// fan-outs. Phase 3 just exposes the raw handles + the
146	/// offer/accept-answer dance.
147	///
148	/// Note: there's no `Session` here. The host doesn't consume the
149	/// `Transport` trait directly — it operates one level lower because
150	/// it needs to tag incoming bytes by `PeerId` and demultiplex
151	/// outgoing bytes back to specific peers' DataChannels (Phase 4
152	/// adds spectator channels).
153	pub struct HostHandshake {
154	    pub pc: RtcPeerConnection,
155	    /// Slot for the host-side DataChannel. Populated synchronously
156	    /// during `connect_as_host` (the host creates the channel before
157	    /// generating the offer); consumers may `clone()` to drive it.
158	    pub dc: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>>,
159	    pub offer: OfferBlob,
160	    /// Reactive view of the joiner's connection state. `Open` once
161	    /// the DataChannel handshake completes; `Closed`/`Error` on
162	    /// failure. Phase 4's `host_room.rs` watches this to know when
163	    /// to emit `Hello` / detect peer disconnect.
164	    pub state: ReadSignal<ConnState>,
165	    /// Wasm-bindgen closures kept alive for the connection's
166	    /// lifetime. Drop the `HostHandshake` and they all get freed.
167	    _keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>,
168	}
169	
170	impl HostHandshake {
171	    /// Apply the joiner's answer SDP to complete the handshake.
172	    ///
173	    /// Pre-flights `signalingState` so we surface "the joiner's
174	    /// answer is stale" / "you accepted twice" before throwing the
175	    /// browser's cryptic `Called in wrong state` error. See
176	    /// `pitfalls/...` (the iOS-backgrounding pitfall, when written).
177	    pub async fn accept_answer(&self, answer: AnswerBlob) -> Result<(), JsValue> {
178	        let signaling = self.pc.signaling_state();
179	        if signaling != RtcSignalingState::HaveLocalOffer {
180	            return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!(
181	                "PeerConnection signaling state is `{signaling:?}` (expected `HaveLocalOffer`). \
182	                 The host's offer may have been replaced (multiple Start hosting taps), \
183	                 the page was paused (iOS Safari backgrounding tears down RTC), or this \
184	                 answer was already accepted."
185	            )));
186	        }
187	        let answer_sdp = decode_answer(&answer)?;
188	        let answer_desc = RtcSessionDescriptionInit::new(RtcSdpType::Answer);
189	        answer_desc.set_sdp(&answer_sdp);
190	        JsFuture::from(self.pc.set_remote_description(&answer_desc)).await?;
191	        Ok(())
192	    }
193	}
194	// SECTION: JoinerHandshake (return value of connect_as_joiner)
195	
196	/// What `connect_as_joiner` returns.
197	///
198	/// `session` is a normal [`Session`] — drop it into `pages/play.rs`
199	/// or `pages/lobby.rs` exactly where the WebSocket `Session` would
200	/// have gone.
201	///
202	/// `answer` is the SDP blob to deliver back to the host out-of-band
203	/// (paste into a textarea / scan via QR / whatever the higher-level
204	/// flow uses).
205	pub struct JoinerHandshake {
206	    pub session: Session,
207	    pub answer: AnswerBlob,
208	    /// `RtcPeerConnection` reference — kept alive in the page so the
209	    /// connection survives. Wraps in `Rc<RefCell<...>>` to match the
210	    /// host side (if a future caller wants to e.g. close it
211	    /// proactively).
212	    pub pc: Rc<RtcPeerConnection>,
213	    _keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>,
214	}
215	// SECTION: connect_as_joiner factory
216	
217	/// Open a WebRTC session as the joiner.
218	///
219	/// Steps:
220	/// 1. Create `RtcPeerConnection` with `cfg.ice_mode`.
221	/// 2. Wire `ondatachannel` so we receive the host's DataChannel.
222	/// 3. Apply the host's offer SDP via `setRemoteDescription`.
223	/// 4. Generate an answer SDP via `createAnswer` + `setLocalDescription`.
224	/// 5. Wait up to [`ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS`] for ICE gathering.
225	/// 6. Return the answer SDP + a [`Session`] backed by the DataChannel.
226	pub async fn connect_as_joiner(
227	    cfg: WebRtcConfig,
228	    offer: OfferBlob,
229	) -> Result<JoinerHandshake, JsValue> {
230	    let pc = new_peer_connection(cfg.ice_mode)?;
231	    let pc_rc = Rc::new(pc);
232	    let dc_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
233	    let mut keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>> = Vec::new();
234	
235	    // `incoming` is a queue (push from DC onmessage, page drains via
236	    // tick signal). See `transport::Incoming` doc for the rationale.
237	    let incoming = Incoming::new();
238	    let (state, set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Connecting);
239	
240	    // The DataChannel arrives on `ondatachannel` after the host's
241	    // SDP is applied. Wire it up before we set the remote description
242	    // so we don't miss the event.
243	    {
244	        let dc_holder = dc_holder.clone();
245	        let incoming_for_dc = incoming.clone();
246	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |ev: JsValue| {
247	            let ev: RtcDataChannelEvent = ev.unchecked_into();
248	            let dc = ev.channel();
249	            install_dc_handlers_for_joiner(&dc, incoming_for_dc.clone(), set_state);
250	            *dc_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(dc);
251	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
252	        pc_rc.set_ondatachannel(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
253	        keepalive.push(cb);
254	    }
255	
256	    let offer_sdp = decode_offer(&offer)?;
257	    let offer_desc = RtcSessionDescriptionInit::new(RtcSdpType::Offer);
258	    offer_desc.set_sdp(&offer_sdp);
259	    JsFuture::from(pc_rc.set_remote_description(&offer_desc)).await?;
260	
261	    let answer = JsFuture::from(pc_rc.create_answer()).await?;
262	    let answer_desc: RtcSessionDescriptionInit = answer.unchecked_into();
263	    JsFuture::from(pc_rc.set_local_description(&answer_desc)).await?;
264	    wait_for_ice_complete(&pc_rc, &mut keepalive).await;
265	
266	    let sdp = pc_rc.local_description().map(|d| d.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
267	    let answer_blob = AnswerBlob(encode_sdp("answer", &sdp));
268	
269	    let handle: Rc<dyn Transport> = Rc::new(WebRtcTransport { dc: dc_holder });
270	    let session = Session { handle, incoming, state };
271	    Ok(JoinerHandshake { session, answer: answer_blob, pc: pc_rc, _keepalive: keepalive })
272	}
273	// SECTION: connect_as_host factory
274	
275	/// Open a WebRTC session as the host.
276	///
277	/// Steps:
278	/// 1. Create `RtcPeerConnection` with `cfg.ice_mode`.
279	/// 2. Create the DataChannel (host always initiates; joiner discovers
280	///    via `ondatachannel`).
281	/// 3. Generate an offer SDP via `createOffer` + `setLocalDescription`.
282	/// 4. Wait up to [`ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS`] for ICE gathering.
283	/// 5. Return the offer SDP. Caller delivers it out-of-band, then calls
284	///    `HostHandshake::accept_answer` with the joiner's response.
285	///
286	/// State signal flips to `Open` when the DataChannel handshake
287	/// completes (on top of `accept_answer` succeeding).
288	pub async fn connect_as_host(cfg: WebRtcConfig) -> Result<HostHandshake, JsValue> {
289	    let pc = new_peer_connection(cfg.ice_mode)?;
290	    let dc_init = {
291	        let init = RtcDataChannelInit::new();
292	        // reliable + ordered — the chess-net wire shape assumes
293	        // in-order delivery.
294	        init.set_ordered(true);
295	        init
296	    };
297	    let dc = pc.create_data_channel_with_data_channel_dict("chess", &dc_init);
298	    let dc_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(Some(dc.clone())));
299	    let mut keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>> = Vec::new();
300	
301	    let (state, set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Connecting);
302	    install_dc_state_handlers(&dc, set_state);
303	
304	    let offer = JsFuture::from(pc.create_offer()).await?;
305	    let offer_desc: RtcSessionDescriptionInit = offer.unchecked_into();
306	    JsFuture::from(pc.set_local_description(&offer_desc)).await?;
307	    wait_for_ice_complete(&pc, &mut keepalive).await;
308	
309	    let sdp = pc.local_description().map(|d| d.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
310	    let offer_blob = OfferBlob(encode_sdp("offer", &sdp));
311	    Ok(HostHandshake { pc, dc: dc_holder, offer: offer_blob, state, _keepalive: keepalive })
312	}
313	/// Block until `dc.ready_state() == "open"` OR `timeout_ms` elapses.
314	/// Returns `true` if the channel opened, `false` on timeout.
315	///
316	/// Used by the host page after `accept_answer` resolves: that
317	/// `await` completes as soon as `setRemoteDescription` returns,
318	/// which is BEFORE the SCTP handshake finishes. Calling
319	/// `dc.send_with_str(...)` on a still-`Connecting` DataChannel
320	/// silently fails (browsers return Err but the chess `PeerSink::Remote`
321	/// path discards the result), so the host's `Hello` + `ChatHistory`
322	/// fanout to the joiner would be lost. Awaiting this helper before
323	/// `HostRoom::attach_remote_player_dc` ensures the fanout reaches
324	/// the joiner.
325	///
326	/// Polls every 50 ms via `setTimeout`. Lower-level alternative would
327	/// be a Promise wrapping `dc.set_onopen`, but we already need the
328	/// timeout path for hostile-network failure cases, so polling is
329	/// simpler than two-channel select.
330	pub async fn wait_for_dc_open(dc: &RtcDataChannel, timeout_ms: u32) -> bool {
331	    let start = js_sys::Date::now();
332	    let deadline = start + timeout_ms as f64;
333	    loop {
334	        if dc.ready_state() == RtcDataChannelState::Open {
335	            return true;
336	        }
337	        if js_sys::Date::now() > deadline {
338	            return false;
339	        }
340	        let promise = js_sys::Promise::new(&mut |resolve, _reject| {
341	            if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
342	                let _ = win.set_timeout_with_callback_and_timeout_and_arguments_0(&resolve, 50);
343	            }
344	        });
345	        let _ = JsFuture::from(promise).await;
346	    }
347	}
348	// SECTION: SDP envelope encode/decode
349	
350	/// Wrap `(type, sdp)` in a JSON envelope so the textarea contents survive
351	/// copy-paste through clipboard / AirDrop / IM apps that helpfully
352	/// "normalise" line endings (SDP per RFC 4566 demands CRLF; many
353	/// transports strip the CR).
354	///
355	/// Pretty-printed with 2-space indent so the contents eyeball the same
356	/// as raw SDP — useful while debugging.
357	pub fn encode_sdp(kind: &str, sdp: &str) -> String {
358	    format!("{{\n  \"type\": \"{}\",\n  \"sdp\": \"{}\"\n}}", kind, escape_json_string(sdp))
359	}
360	
361	fn escape_json_string(s: &str) -> String {
362	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(s.len() + 8);
363	    for ch in s.chars() {
364	        match ch {
365	            '"' => out.push_str("\\\""),
366	            '\\' => out.push_str("\\\\"),
367	            '\n' => out.push_str("\\n"),
368	            '\r' => out.push_str("\\r"),
369	            '\t' => out.push_str("\\t"),
370	            c if (c as u32) < 0x20 => {
371	                out.push_str(&format!("\\u{:04x}", c as u32));
372	            }
373	            c => out.push(c),
374	        }
375	    }
376	    out
377	}
378	
379	fn decode_sdp_envelope(blob: &str) -> Result<(String, String), JsValue> {
380	    let trimmed = blob.trim();
381	    if !trimmed.starts_with('{') {
382	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(
383	            "SDP must be the JSON envelope produced by the host page (raw-SDP is not accepted)",
384	        ));
385	    }
386	    let parsed = js_sys::JSON::parse(trimmed)
387	        .map_err(|e| JsValue::from_str(&format!("SDP envelope is not valid JSON: {e:?}")))?;
388	    let kind =
389	        Reflect::get(&parsed, &"type".into()).ok().and_then(|v| v.as_string()).unwrap_or_default();
390	    let sdp =
391	        Reflect::get(&parsed, &"sdp".into()).ok().and_then(|v| v.as_string()).unwrap_or_default();
392	    if kind.is_empty() || sdp.is_empty() {
393	        return Err(JsValue::from_str("SDP envelope JSON missing `type` or `sdp` field"));
394	    }
395	    // Browsers accept LF-only SDP in practice but normalise anyway —
396	    // copy-paste through AirDrop / SMS / IM may strip CR.
397	    let sdp = sdp.replace('\r', "").replace('\n', "\r\n");
398	    Ok((kind, sdp))
399	}
400	
401	fn decode_offer(blob: &OfferBlob) -> Result<String, JsValue> {
402	    let (kind, sdp) = decode_sdp_envelope(&blob.0)?;
403	    if kind != "offer" {
404	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!("expected offer envelope, got `{kind}`")));
405	    }
406	    Ok(sdp)
407	}
408	
409	fn decode_answer(blob: &AnswerBlob) -> Result<String, JsValue> {
410	    let (kind, sdp) = decode_sdp_envelope(&blob.0)?;
411	    if kind != "answer" {
412	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!("expected answer envelope, got `{kind}`")));
413	    }
414	    Ok(sdp)
415	}
416	// SECTION: ICE config + ICE-gather wait helper
417	
418	fn new_peer_connection(mode: IceMode) -> Result<RtcPeerConnection, JsValue> {
419	    let cfg = RtcConfiguration::new();
420	    let servers: Array = Array::new();
421	    if mode == IceMode::WithStun {
422	        // Multi-server STUN list, ordered for CN reachability:
423	        //   - stun.miwifi.com — Xiaomi's own STUN; reachable from CN.
424	        //   - stun.qq.com     — Tencent's; reachable from CN.
425	        //   - stun.cloudflare.com — Cloudflare; usually reachable.
426	        //   - stun.l.google.com — global default; BLOCKED IN CN by GFW.
427	        // Browsers race them in parallel and use whichever responds
428	        // first; the GFW-blocked Google entry is harmless because the
429	        // others answer first.
430	        for url in [
431	            "stun:stun.miwifi.com:3478",
432	            "stun:stun.qq.com:3478",
433	            "stun:stun.cloudflare.com:3478",
434	            "stun:stun.l.google.com:19302",
435	        ] {
436	            let server = js_sys::Object::new();
437	            Reflect::set(&server, &"urls".into(), &url.into()).ok();
438	            servers.push(&server);
439	        }
440	    }
441	    Reflect::set(&cfg, &"iceServers".into(), &servers).ok();
442	    RtcPeerConnection::new_with_configuration(&cfg)
443	}
444	
445	/// Block until `pc.iceGatheringState == "complete"`, OR
446	/// [`ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS`] elapses (whichever comes first).
447	async fn wait_for_ice_complete(
448	    pc: &RtcPeerConnection,
449	    keepalive: &mut Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>,
450	) {
451	    if pc.ice_gathering_state() == RtcIceGatheringState::Complete {
452	        return;
453	    }
454	    let promise = js_sys::Promise::new(&mut |resolve, _reject| {
455	        let pc_inner = pc.clone();
456	        let resolve_for_state = resolve.clone();
457	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
458	            if pc_inner.ice_gathering_state() == RtcIceGatheringState::Complete {
459	                let _ = resolve_for_state.call0(&JsValue::NULL);
460	            }
461	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
462	        pc.set_onicegatheringstatechange(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
463	        keepalive.push(cb);
464	
465	        // Hard-cap the wait. Late `complete` events still fire but
466	        // promise resolution is idempotent.
467	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
468	            let resolve_for_timeout = resolve.clone();
469	            let pc_for_timeout = pc.clone();
470	            let timeout_cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
471	                web_sys::console::warn_2(
472	                    &"[webrtc] ICE gather timeout — proceeding with whatever candidates we have. Final state:".into(),
473	                    &format!("{:?}", pc_for_timeout.ice_gathering_state()).into(),
474	                );
475	                let _ = resolve_for_timeout.call0(&JsValue::NULL);
476	            }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
477	            let _ = win.set_timeout_with_callback_and_timeout_and_arguments_0(
478	                timeout_cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref(),
479	                ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS,
480	            );
481	            keepalive.push(timeout_cb);
482	        }
483	    });
484	    let _ = JsFuture::from(promise).await;
485	}
486	// SECTION: DataChannel handler installation
487	
488	/// Joiner-side handlers: incoming bytes deserialize as `ServerMsg`,
489	/// `onopen` flips the state signal to `Open`, `onclose` to `Closed`.
490	fn install_dc_handlers_for_joiner(
491	    dc: &RtcDataChannel,
492	    incoming: Incoming,
493	    state: WriteSignal<ConnState>,
494	) {
495	    {
496	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
497	            state.set(ConnState::Open);
498	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
499	        dc.set_onopen(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
500	        // Closures here intentionally leak — they have to outlive the
501	        // PeerConnection. The browser only fires `onopen` once + a
502	        // bounded number of `onmessage`/`onclose` events; in steady
503	        // state of an open connection the closures are kept alive by
504	        // the JS side via the listener registration. The page's
505	        // `JoinerHandshake._keepalive` keeps the gathering closures
506	        // alive separately.
507	        cb.forget();
508	    }
509	    {
510	        let incoming_for_msg = incoming.clone();
511	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |ev: JsValue| {
512	            let ev: MessageEvent = ev.unchecked_into();
513	            if let Some(text) = ev.data().as_string() {
514	                if let Ok(msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMsg>(&text) {
515	                    incoming_for_msg.push(msg);
516	                }
517	            }
518	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
519	        dc.set_onmessage(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
520	        cb.forget();
521	    }
522	    {
523	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
524	            state.set(ConnState::Closed);
525	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
526	        dc.set_onclose(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
527	        cb.forget();
528	    }
529	
530	    // Defensive: if the DataChannel is ALREADY open by the time we
531	    // set the handlers (fast LAN path — the SCTP handshake can
532	    // complete in <1 ms, faster than `ondatachannel` → handler
533	    // installation Rust closures), the `onopen` event has already
534	    // fired and we'd never see it. Manually flip the state signal
535	    // so the page can transition. Browsers don't re-fire `onopen`.
536	    if dc.ready_state() == RtcDataChannelState::Open {
537	        state.set(ConnState::Open);
538	    }
539	}
540	
541	/// Host-side state handlers — flip `state` to `Open`/`Closed` based on
542	/// DataChannel events. `onmessage` is wired separately by Phase 4's
543	/// `host_room.rs` because incoming bytes for the host are
544	/// `ClientMsg`, not `ServerMsg`, and they need to be tagged with a
545	/// `PeerId` before going to `Room::apply`.
546	fn install_dc_state_handlers(dc: &RtcDataChannel, state: WriteSignal<ConnState>) {
547	    {
548	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
549	            state.set(ConnState::Open);
550	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
551	        dc.set_onopen(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
552	        cb.forget();
553	    }
554	    {
555	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
556	            state.set(ConnState::Closed);
557	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
558	        dc.set_onclose(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
559	        cb.forget();
560	    }
561	    // Defensive (see `install_dc_handlers_for_joiner` for the
562	    // rationale): cover the race where the DC opens before the
563	    // handler is installed.
564	    if dc.ready_state() == RtcDataChannelState::Open {
565	        state.set(ConnState::Open);
566	    }
567	}
568	
569	// SECTION: tests
570	//
571	// The async factories — `connect_as_joiner` / `connect_as_host` —
572	// require a live `RtcPeerConnection` and are exercised end-to-end via
573	// the spike pages on real devices. Mocking `RtcPeerConnection` for
574	// unit tests has historically been more pain than insight.
575	//
576	// The pure-string SDP envelope codec is small enough that
577	// `pages/play.rs` exercising it through `connect_as_joiner` (which it
578	// will once Phase 4 wires this up) is adequate coverage. Phase 5 may
579	// add a `wasm_bindgen_test` harness once the page-level QR code
580	// paths land.
581
```

1	//! `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` routes — LAN multiplayer over WebRTC.
2	//!
3	//! Phase 5 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Pairs two browsers
4	//! on the same WiFi via the Phase 3 `transport::webrtc` plumbing
5	//! and the Phase 4 `host_room::HostRoom` authority, then drops the
6	//! resulting `Session` into the existing `pages/play::PlayConnected`
7	//! so the actual game UI is unchanged from chess-net mode.
8	//!
9	//! No QR / camera scanning yet — Phase 5 v1 uses textareas + the
10	//! browser clipboard for the offer/answer exchange. QR is
11	//! deferred (Phase 5.5 if requested).

39	pub fn LanHostPage() -> impl IntoView {
40	    // ── Page state ────────────────────────────────────────────
41	    // We progress through:
42	    //   Idle → Generating → AwaitingAnswer → AcceptingAnswer →
43	    //   WaitingForChannel → Playing
44	    // Each transition mutates the corresponding signals; the view
45	    // renders accordingly.
46	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<HostStatus>(HostStatus::Idle);
47	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
48	    let (offer_blob, set_offer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
49	    let (answer_input, set_answer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
50	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
51	
52	    // QR scanner modal state. `cam_available` is set asynchronously
53	    // on mount; default false to keep the Scan-camera button hidden
54	    // until detection completes (no flash-of-button-then-disappear
55	    // for cameraless devices).
56	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
57	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
58	    spawn_local(async move {
59	        if has_camera().await {
60	            set_cam_available.set(true);
61	        }
62	    });
63	
64	    // Keep the in-flight HostHandshake alive in the page; closures
65	    // and accept_answer need a stable reference.
66	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<HostHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
67	    // The HostRoom + its Session aren't constructed until the
68	    // DataChannel actually opens; on `state == Open` we instantiate
69	    // both and flip status to `Playing`.
70	    let host_room: Rc<RefCell<Option<Rc<HostRoom>>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
71	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
72	
73	    // ── Open room (generate offer) ────────────────────────────
74	    let handshake_for_open = handshake.clone();
75	    let on_open: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
76	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::Idle) {
77	            return;
78	        }
79	        set_error_msg.set(None);
80	        set_status.set(HostStatus::Generating);
81	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
82	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
83	        };
84	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_open.clone();
85	        spawn_local(async move {
86	            match connect_as_host(cfg).await {
87	                Ok(hh) => {
88	                    set_offer_blob.set(hh.offer.0.clone());
89	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(hh);
90	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer);
91	                }
92	                Err(e) => {
93	                    set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("connect_as_host failed: {e:?}")));
94	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
95	                }
96	            }
97	        });
98	    });
99	
100	    // ── Accept answer ──────────────────────────────────────────
101	    let handshake_for_accept = handshake.clone();
102	    let host_room_slot = host_room.clone();
103	    let on_accept: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
104	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer) {
105	            return;
106	        }
107	        let blob = answer_input.get_untracked();
108	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
109	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the joiner's answer SDP first".into()));
110	            return;
111	        }
112	        set_error_msg.set(None);
113	        set_status.set(HostStatus::AcceptingAnswer);
114	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_accept.clone();
115	        let host_room_slot = host_room_slot.clone();
116	        spawn_local(async move {
117	            let hh = match handshake_slot.borrow_mut().take() {
118	                Some(hh) => hh,
119	                None => {
120	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("no handshake in flight".into()));
121	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
122	                    return;
123	                }
124	            };
125	            if let Err(e) = hh.accept_answer(AnswerBlob(blob)).await {
126	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("accept_answer failed: {e:?}")));
127	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
128	                return;
129	            }
130	            // CRITICAL: `accept_answer` resolves as soon as
131	            // `setRemoteDescription` returns — BEFORE the SCTP
132	            // handshake completes. If we call `attach_remote_player_dc`
133	            // immediately, the `dc.send_with_str(...)` for Hello +
134	            // ChatHistory silently fails (DC ready_state is still
135	            // "connecting"). Wait for actual DC open first.
136	            let dc = match hh.dc.borrow().clone() {
137	                Some(d) => d,
138	                None => {
139	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("DataChannel slot is empty".into()));
140	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
141	                    return;
142	                }
143	            };
144	            if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
145	                set_error_msg.set(Some(
146	                    "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)"
147	                        .into(),
148	                ));
149	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
150	                return;
151	            }
152	            let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, /* hints */ false);
153	            if let Err(e) = room.attach_remote_player_dc(dc) {
154	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("attach joiner failed: {e:?}")));
155	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
156	                return;
157	            }
158	            *host_room_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(room);
159	            set_play_session.set(Some(session));
160	            set_status.set(HostStatus::Playing);
161	        });
162	    });
163	
164	    // ── Reset (full page reload) ──────────────────────────────
165	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
166	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
167	            let _ = win.location().reload();
168	        }
169	    });
170	
171	    view! {
172	        <div class="lan-page" style="max-width:720px;margin:1.5rem auto;padding:1rem">
173	            <Show
174	                when=move || matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Playing)
175	                fallback=move || view! {

177	                    <p class="muted">

182	                    <p>

186	                        >

188	                        </button>
189	                        <button on:click=move |_| on_reset.call(()) style="margin-left:0.5rem">
190	                            "Reset"
191	                        </button>
192	                        <label style="margin-left:1rem;font-size:13px">
193	                            <input
194	                                type="checkbox"
195	                                prop:checked=move || use_stun.get()
196	                                disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Idle)
197	                                on:change=move |ev| {
198	                                    let v = ev.target()
199	                                        .and_then(|t| t.dyn_into::<web_sys::HtmlInputElement>().ok())
200	                                        .map(|el| el.checked())
201	                                        .unwrap_or(false);
202	                                    set_use_stun.set(v);
203	                                }
204	                            />
205	                            " Use STUN (workaround for hostile networks)"
206	                        </label>
207	                    </p>
208	                    <p>"Status: " {move || format!("{:?}", status.get())}</p>
209	                    <Show when=move || !offer_blob.with(|s| s.is_empty())>
210	                        <p>"Have the joiner scan this QR — or copy the text and AirDrop / Messages it:"</p>
211	                        <QrCodeView
212	                            payload=Signal::derive(move || offer_blob.get())
213	                            label="LAN host offer SDP"
214	                        />
215	                        <p style="margin-top:0.75rem">"Or copy as text:"</p>
216	                        <textarea
217	                            rows="6"
218	                            readonly=true
219	                            style="width:100%;font-family:monospace;font-size:12px"
220	                            prop:value=move || offer_blob.get()
221	                        />
222	                        <p>
223	                            <button on:click=move |_| {
224	                                let blob = offer_blob.get();
225	                                if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
226	                                    let _ = win.navigator().clipboard().write_text(&blob);
227	                                }

231	                            <span style="margin-left:0.5rem;font-size:12px;color:#666">
232	                                "Bytes: " {move || offer_blob.with(|s| s.len())}
233	                            </span>
234	                        </p>
235	                        <p>"2. Get the joiner's answer back. Either scan their QR with the camera, or paste their text below:"</p>
236	                        <Show when=move || cam_available.get()>

238	                                <button on:click=move |_| set_scanner_open.set(true)>

241	                            </p>
242	                        </Show>

299	    Generating,

307	pub fn LanJoinPage() -> impl IntoView {
308	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<JoinStatus>(JoinStatus::Idle);
309	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
310	    let (offer_input, set_offer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
311	    let (answer_blob, set_answer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
312	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
313	
314	    // QR scanner modal state. See LanHostPage for the same pattern.
315	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
316	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
317	    spawn_local(async move {
318	        if has_camera().await {
319	            set_cam_available.set(true);
320	        }
321	    });
322	
323	    // Joiner side: store the JoinerHandshake so we can pull its
324	    // session out once the DC opens.
325	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<JoinerHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
326	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
327	
328	    // Holder pattern: spawn_local runs without a Leptos owner context,
329	    // so a `create_effect` inside it would be GC'd immediately (no
330	    // subscriptions retained — `state.set(Open)` would fire but no
331	    // effect would react). Solution: define the holder signals and
332	    // the effect at component scope (properly owned), and have
333	    // spawn_local just `set` the holders. The component-scope effect
334	    // re-runs when the holders change AND when the inner state
335	    // signal changes.
336	    let (joiner_state_holder, set_joiner_state_holder) =
337	        create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<ConnState>>>(None);
338	    let session_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<Session>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
339	
340	    {
341	        let session_holder = session_holder.clone();
342	        create_effect(move |_| {
343	            // Read both signals to register subscriptions:
344	            //   * joiner_state_holder fires once when handshake completes.
345	            //   * the inner state signal fires when DC opens.
346	            if let Some(state_sig) = joiner_state_holder.get() {
347	                if state_sig.get() == ConnState::Open {
348	                    if let Some(session) = session_holder.borrow().clone() {
349	                        set_play_session.set(Some(session));
350	                        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Playing);
351	                    }
352	                }
353	            }
354	        });
355	    }

357	    let handshake_for_gen = handshake.clone();
358	    let session_holder_for_gen = session_holder.clone();
359	    let on_generate: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
360	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), JoinStatus::Idle) {
361	            return;
362	        }
363	        let blob = offer_input.get_untracked();
364	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
365	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the host's offer SDP first".into()));
366	            return;
367	        }
368	        set_error_msg.set(None);
369	        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Generating);
370	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {

```text
# Trunk dev-server config. The single `/ws` proxy entry covers BOTH
# `/ws` (default-room upgrade for v1 chess-tui clients) and `/ws/<room>`
# (named room) because Trunk nests the proxy as a prefix route in axum.
# Registering separate `/ws` and `/ws/` entries panics on startup with
# "conflict with previously registered route" — see the gotcha note in
# `docs/trunk-leptos-wasm.md`.
#
# `addresses` is the new (Trunk 0.21+) replacement for the deprecated
# `address` scalar. Production-mode prod is chess-net's `--static-dir`
# path; Trunk is dev-only.

[build]
target  = "index.html"
release = false

# Post-build hook: render `dist/sw.js` and `dist/manifest.webmanifest`
# from the templates in `public/`. The hook scans dist/ for hashed
# JS/WASM/CSS asset names and bakes them into the SW precache list.
# See `scripts/build-pwa.sh` for the substitution rules.
[[hooks]]
stage = "post_build"
command = "bash"
command_arguments = ["scripts/build-pwa.sh"]

[serve]
addresses = ["127.0.0.1"]
port      = 8080
open      = false

# WebSocket upgrades — chess-net's /ws and /ws/<room> routes.
[[proxy]]
backend = "ws://127.0.0.1:7878/ws"
ws      = true
rewrite = "/ws"

# Lobby push channel.
[[proxy]]
backend = "ws://127.0.0.1:7878/lobby"
ws      = true
rewrite = "/lobby"

# Snapshot endpoint (debug; the SPA itself uses the lobby ws).
[[proxy]]
backend = "http://127.0.0.1:7878/rooms"
rewrite = "/rooms"
```
</details></tool-use>

52	        path

54	        format!("{base}/")
55	    } else {

57	    }

60	fn normalize_base_path(base: &str) -> String {
61	    let trimmed = base.trim().trim_matches('/');
62	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
63	        String::new()
64	    } else {
65	        format!("/{trimmed}")
66	    }
67	}

```text
1	# WebRTC mDNS `.local` candidates fail to connect across LAN devices
2	
3	(file slug kept as `webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` for cross-link
4	stability; original "AP isolation" hypothesis was wrong, see Root cause
5	section below for the corrected story.)
6	
7	## Verbatim symptom
8	
9	Two browsers on the same WiFi network exchange WebRTC SDP successfully
10	(`signalingState: stable` on both sides) but the connection never opens:
11	
12	- Host shows `iceConnectionState: Checking → Failed` (or `Disconnected`).
13	- DataChannel `onopen` never fires.
14	- No console errors — ICE just times out silently.
15	
16	Each peer's SDP contains exactly one host candidate with a `<uuid>.local`
17	mDNS hostname:
18	
19	```
20	a=candidate:1922153879 1 udp 2113937151 134f414a-126d-4995-be11-f07d6ec0b725.local 59843 typ host generation 0 network-cost 999
21	```
22	
23	Hits both Safari and Chrome on iOS / iPadOS; macOS Safari ↔ macOS Safari
24	in the same machine works fine. So this is a network-path issue, not
25	browser-specific.
26	
27	**Confirmed reproduction** (2026-05-12): macOS Safari host + iPad
28	Safari/Chrome joiner on a Xiaomi AX9000 (firmware MiWiFi 1.0.168 CN,
29	subnet 192.168.31.x) → Disconnected. **Same two devices on the iPhone's
30	personal hotspot → Connected in <1 second.** The router is the variable.
31	
32	## Root cause
33	
34	Modern browsers (Chrome ≥ 76, Safari ≥ 13.x) hide LAN IP addresses
35	behind randomised `<uuid>.local` mDNS hostnames in WebRTC ICE candidates
36	as a fingerprinting-prevention measure
37	([Chrome announcement](https://groups.google.com/g/discuss-webrtc/c/6stQXi72BEU)).
38	Each peer must resolve the OTHER's `<uuid>.local` hostname via mDNS
39	(UDP 5353 / 224.0.0.251) before it can attempt connectivity.
40	
41	**The original guess on this network was "router blocks mDNS multicast"
42	— that turned out to be WRONG, see "What we ruled out" below.** The
43	real failure mode appears to be more subtle and is still under
44	investigation. The symptom + workaround menu still apply, but the root
45	cause of *why* the iPhone hotspot succeeds where the home WiFi fails is
46	not "AP isolation".
47	
48	## What we ruled out (and the evidence)
49	
50	Hypothesis | Status | Evidence
51	---|---|---
52	**"Router does AP / Client / Wireless Isolation"** | ❌ disproven | Mac's `tcpdump -i en0 -n udp port 5353` captures live mDNS traffic from other LAN devices: `_miio._udp.local.` from Mi IoT (192.168.31.63 lumi-acpartner-v2), `_airplay._tcp.local.` + `_raop._tcp.local.` TXT records from a 192.168.31.188 iPad Pro AirPlay receiver, IPv6 mDNS on `ff02::fb` too. Multicast bridges fine across the router's 2.4G/5G/5G-Game radios into one `br-lan`. `dns-sd -B _services._dns-sd._udp local.` discovers cross-device services normally.
53	**"Router blocks all multicast"** | ❌ disproven | Same evidence — multicast clearly works.
54	**"Devices on different VLANs / SSIDs can't see each other"** | ❌ disproven | All three SSIDs (different names per band) bridge to the same `br-lan`. iPad ↔ Mac AirPlay handshake works, which is itself mDNS-driven service discovery + UDP P2P.
55	**"Xiaomi router has a hidden AP-isolation toggle in admin UI"** | ❌ disproven | Inspected every page in 高级設置 (QoS / DDNS / 端口转发 / VPN / 其他) and 常用設置 (Wi-Fi / 上网 / 安全中心 / 局域网 / 系统状态). Only mDNS-relevant toggles surfaced are `miotrelay` (Mi IoT relay) and `miscan` (5G AIoT scan), both ON. No AP-isolation control. Stock firmware; SSH/Telnet disabled (no shell access to inspect `ebtables` / `brctl` / wireless driver multicast-snooping state).
56	
57	## What's actually happening (best current hypothesis)
58	
59	General mDNS service discovery (`_airplay._tcp`, `_miio._udp`, `_raop._tcp`)
60	works on this LAN — confirmed by tcpdump and `dns-sd`. WebRTC's
61	`<uuid>.local` resolution specifically does NOT work between Mac and
62	iPad. The differences between general mDNS service discovery and
63	WebRTC's hostname resolution that could matter:
64	
65	1. **WebRTC mDNS hostnames are dynamically registered per session.**
66	   Browsers generate a fresh UUID per ICE-gathering pass, register it
67	   with the OS-level mDNS responder (Bonjour on macOS, similar on iOS),
68	   and unregister it when the PeerConnection closes. There's a small
69	   window between "hostname appears in SDP and is sent to peer" and
70	   "responder advertises it on the network". On a busy / Mi-relayed
71	   network the timing might be fragile.
72	2. **AWDL interference.** Apple devices use AWDL (`awdl0` on the Mac)
73	   for direct Apple↔Apple P2P (AirDrop, AirPlay etc.). The Mac's mDNS
74	   responder advertises on both `awdl0` and `en0`. WebRTC's mDNS
75	   resolution may prefer `awdl0` for `<uuid>.local` queries from another
76	   Apple device, and AWDL may not bridge through the router the same
77	   way regular Wi-Fi does. Possibly relevant: hotspot LAN doesn't have
78	   the same AWDL ambiguity (the iPhone IS the gateway).
79	3. **`network-cost: 999`** in candidates suggests browsers tag this
80	   network as "expensive" — possibly affecting candidate priority but
81	   shouldn't kill the connection.
82	4. **Mi router `miotrelay` (畅快连)** — Mi's own multicast relay
83	   daemon. It bridges Mi IoT discovery across radios, but its semantics
84	   for non-Mi `<uuid>.local` traffic are undocumented; it may rewrite
85	   or filter packets in ways that confuse browser mDNS responders.
86	   No SSH access means we can't inspect what it actually does.
87	5. **Double NAT layer** at 192.168.1.1 → 192.168.31.1 shouldn't matter
88	   (mDNS is link-local) but adds another variable.
89	
90	This is the part the spike's data points have NOT yet isolated. The
91	practical mitigation menu still works whatever the actual cause is.
92	
93	## Workarounds
94	
95	### A. Switch to a phone hotspot (works immediately, confirmed 2026-05-12)
96	
97	If both devices can connect to the *same* iPhone / Android personal
98	hotspot, that's a guaranteed-clean LAN. Confirmed working with the
99	chess-web Phase 0 spike (LanOnly mode, no STUN) — DataChannel opens
100	within 1 second of accepting the answer.
101	
102	This is also the recommended workaround for friends visiting your
103	home who can't reconfigure your router: one of you turns on personal
104	hotspot and the other joins it.
105	
106	### B. Add STUN with CN-reachable servers (diagnostic / fallback)
107	
108	Pass `iceServers: [{...}]` with one or more STUN servers to
109	`RtcPeerConnection`. Browsers then publish `srflx` (server-reflexive)
110	candidates with the public IP as seen by the STUN server. Two peers
111	behind the same NAT can sometimes reach each other via NAT hairpin,
112	though it's not guaranteed.
113	
114	**STUN-server choice matters in mainland China**: `stun.l.google.com`
115	is on Google infrastructure and is **blocked by the GFW**. The
116	browser's gathering loop hangs waiting for a response that never
117	arrives — `iceGatheringState` sits at `gathering` forever. The
118	`clients/chess-web/src/spike/rtc.rs` configuration uses a multi-server
119	list including `stun.miwifi.com:3478` (Xiaomi), `stun.qq.com:3478`
120	(Tencent), `stun.cloudflare.com:3478` (Cloudflare), with Google STUN
121	as last resort. **The spike's `wait_for_ice_complete` also caps the
122	wait at 5 seconds** so a dead STUN server can't hang the page (see
123	the `ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS` constant).
124	
125	### C. Get `getUserMedia` permission to expose real LAN IPs
126	
127	Granting microphone / camera permission to a page disables the mDNS
128	hostname obfuscation (browsers reason: "if you got mic permission you
129	already have the user's trust, so the small extra IP-leak is moot").
130	Real `host` candidates with the LAN IP appear instead of `.local`.
131	
132	This works but is **wildly user-hostile** — asking for mic permission
133	just to start a chess game is a UX nonstarter. Filed for completeness;
134	not a real fix.
135	
136	### D. Approach C from `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` — LAN signalling helper
137	
138	For users on networks where (A) is impractical and (B) doesn't help
139	enough, fall back to a small chess-net endpoint that brokers the SDP
140	exchange + candidate forwarding. This defeats the "no server"
141	promise but works on any network. Phase 5 follow-up if the spike
142	reveals the WebRTC-mDNS failure mode is too common in the wild.
143	
144	### E. Use a router that exposes peer-to-peer multicast / WebRTC mDNS
145	
146	OpenWRT, Asus Merlin firmware, OPNsense, mikroTik, and most prosumer
147	routers either bridge mDNS by default or have explicit toggles. If
148	the user has admin access and the will to flash, OpenWRT on a Xiaomi
149	router is well-documented. **However, given that general mDNS
150	already works on this Xiaomi stock firmware**, this might NOT actually
151	fix the WebRTC-specific symptom — the failure mode here is something
152	narrower than "router blocks multicast". Test before committing to a
153	flash.
154	
155	## Prevention
156	
157	`backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` Phase 0 spike findings explicitly call
158	out the `Disconnected` / `Failed` ICE-connection-state symptoms so the
159	next agent debugging this knows to:
160	
161	1. **Try the iPhone-hotspot workaround first** (15-second triage that
162	   confirms or kills the network-blocking-WebRTC-mDNS hypothesis).
163	2. **Don't assume "router blocks mDNS"** without tcpdump evidence —
164	   on the AX9000 reproduction, general mDNS clearly works (Mi IoT,
165	   AirPlay TXT records all visible) but WebRTC's `<uuid>.local`
166	   resolution still fails. The failure mode is something narrower
167	   than "no multicast at all".
168	3. Check that any STUN diagnostic uses CN-reachable servers
169	   (`stun.miwifi.com` / `stun.qq.com` / `stun.cloudflare.com` are in
170	   the spike's default list; Google STUN is blocked from CN).
171	4. Recognise that "no AP-isolation toggle visible" doesn't tell you
172	   anything either way — Xiaomi MiWiFi consumer firmware doesn't
173	   expose one even when peer-to-peer multicast IS bridging fine.
174	
175	The `lan_echo.rs` warning banner mentions both the iOS-backgrounding
176	gotcha (separate issue) and points users at the iPhone-hotspot
177	workaround if connection fails.
178	
179	## Useful diagnostic commands (macOS)
180	
181	```sh
182	# Live mDNS browse on en0 — should show cross-device services on a healthy LAN
183	dns-sd -B _services._dns-sd._udp local.
184	
185	# Resolve a specific .local hostname to IP — should succeed for any device
186	# advertising itself; will TIMEOUT (not error) if the responder isn't reachable
187	dns-sd -G v4 some-uuid.local
188	
189	# Capture all mDNS traffic on en0 for ~30s — count cross-device packets
190	sudo tcpdump -i en0 -n udp port 5353 -G 30 -W 1
191	
192	# Capture IPv6 mDNS too
193	sudo tcpdump -i en0 'ip6 and udp port 5353' -G 30 -W 1
194	```
195	
196	For Xiaomi MiWiFi routers the user maintains a read-only LuCI JSON API
197	CLI at `~/bin/mi-router` (`mi-router info / wifi / devices / mdns / raw
198	<endpoint>`); useful for inspecting router state without SSH access.
199
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/pwa-base-path-and-stale-cache.md`

```text
1	# PWA on GitHub Pages subpath: install fails or app shows stale code
2	
3	**Symptoms** (any of):
4	
5	```
6	Chrome DevTools → Application → Manifest:
7	  Error: "Page does not work offline" / "Site cannot be installed"
8	  start_url is not in scope
9	```
10	
11	```
12	Installed PWA opens to a blank page or "Offline and no cached
13	shell available."
14	```
15	
16	```
17	After deploying a new build, users keep seeing the old version
18	for hours / days. Hard reload (Cmd-Shift-R) "fixes" it but a
19	normal reload does not.
20	```
21	
22	```
23	sw.js: Failed to fetch / 404
24	manifest.webmanifest: Failed to fetch / 404
25	```
26	
27	These are three different traps that all happen when a Rust/WASM
28	PWA is deployed under a non-root path (e.g. GitHub Pages at
29	`/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/`). They are easy to misdiagnose because
30	the dev-server flow (Trunk on `localhost:8080/`) works fine.
31	
32	---
33	
34	## Trap 1 — manifest `start_url` / `scope` written without the base path
35	
36	**Symptom**: Chrome refuses to install ("start_url is not in scope")
37	or installs but the launched app loads a blank page.
38	
39	**Root cause**: A static `manifest.webmanifest` baked at compile
40	time with `"start_url": "/"` is wrong on GitHub Pages, where the
41	app actually lives at `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/`. The manifest's
42	URL fields must include the deploy subpath, *and* the `scope`
43	must encompass `start_url`, *and* `start_url` must resolve to a
44	real document on the server.
45	
46	**Fix**: Treat the manifest as a build artifact, not source. We
47	keep `public/manifest.webmanifest.tmpl` with `__BASE_PATH__`
48	placeholders and the post-build hook (`scripts/build-pwa.sh`)
49	substitutes `TRUNK_PUBLIC_URL` (minus its trailing slash) before
50	writing `dist/manifest.webmanifest`. For the GitHub Pages target
51	that produces:
52	
53	```json
54	"start_url": "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/",
55	"scope":     "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/",
56	"id":        "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/"
57	```
58	
59	The `id` field stabilises the install identity across deploys
60	that change the start URL (e.g. moving from a subpath to the
61	root domain) — useful for analytics, harmless to set to the same
62	value as `start_url`.
63	
64	The same hook also rewrites every `icons[].src` to start with
65	the base path. Hard-coded `/icons/icon-512.png` breaks; use
66	`__BASE_PATH__/icons/icon-512.png`.
67	
68	**Verification**:
69	
70	```
71	make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi
72	grep -E '"(start_url|scope|src)"' clients/chess-web/dist-static/manifest.webmanifest
73	```
74	
75	Every URL should start with `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/`.
76	
77	---
78	
79	## Trap 2 — `sw.js` cached by HTTP, new builds invisible for hours
80
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./clients/chess-web/scripts/build-pwa.sh`

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Trunk post-build hook: generate `dist/sw.js` and
3	# `dist/manifest.webmanifest` from the templates in `public/`.
4	#
5	# Trunk passes:
6	#   TRUNK_STAGING_DIR  — absolute path to the dist directory
7	#   TRUNK_PUBLIC_URL   — public URL prefix from `--public-url`
8	#                        (defaults to "/" when not set; for GitHub
9	#                        Pages we run with --public-url "/<repo>/").
10	#
11	# Why a post-build step?
12	#   - Trunk hashes JS/WASM/CSS for cache busting; we want those exact
13	#     hashed filenames in the SW precache list, so we can only know
14	#     them after the build runs.
15	#   - The manifest needs `start_url` / `scope` baked at build-time to
16	#     match the deployment base path (server vs. GitHub Pages subpath).
17	#
18	# This script intentionally avoids any external deps beyond bash + sed
19	# + find + shasum — all default on macOS / Linux dev machines.
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	STAGING="${TRUNK_STAGING_DIR:-clients/chess-web/dist}"
24	PUBLIC_URL="${TRUNK_PUBLIC_URL:-/}"
25	
26	# Normalize PUBLIC_URL → BASE_PATH:
27	#   "/"                           -> ""
28	#   "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/"     -> "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi"
29	#   "Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/"      -> "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi"  (lenient on
30	#                                  WEB_BASE without leading slash)
31	#   "/sub/"                       -> "/sub"
32	BASE_PATH="${PUBLIC_URL%/}"
33	if [[ -n "${BASE_PATH}" && "${BASE_PATH}" != /* ]]; then
34	  BASE_PATH="/${BASE_PATH}"
35	fi
36	
37	if [[ ! -d "${STAGING}" ]]; then
38	  echo "[build-pwa] staging dir missing: ${STAGING}" >&2
39	  exit 1
40	fi
41	
42	# Locate the template sources. Trunk copies them into dist via the
43	# index.html `<link data-trunk rel="copy-file" …>` directives, so the
44	# real source-of-truth is the project tree.
45	HOOK_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
46	PROJECT_DIR="$(cd "${HOOK_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
47	SW_TMPL="${PROJECT_DIR}/public/sw.js.tmpl"
48	MF_TMPL="${PROJECT_DIR}/public/manifest.webmanifest.tmpl"
49	
50	if [[ ! -f "${SW_TMPL}" || ! -f "${MF_TMPL}" ]]; then
51	  echo "[build-pwa] template files missing under ${PROJECT_DIR}/public/" >&2
52	  exit 1
53	fi
54	
55	# ---- Build the precache URL list ------------------------------------
56	#
57	# Walk dist/ and collect every shipping asset. Skip anything that
58	# (a) the SW would refuse to fetch anyway (sw.js itself, sw.js.tmpl /
59	# manifest.webmanifest.tmpl leftovers from the copy-file step), or
60	# (b) is purely social-card metadata not needed offline (og-image.png,
61	# 404.html — GitHub Pages uses 404.html only on real not-founds, and
62	# the SW handles SPA fallback navigation independently).
63	
64	cd "${STAGING}"
65	
66	# Collect relative paths, prefix with BASE_PATH/, sort for determinism.
67	mapfile -t PRECACHE_REL < <(
68	  find . -type f \
69	    \( -name "*.js" -o -name "*.wasm" -o -name "*.css" \
70	       -o -name "*.html" -o -name "*.svg" -o -name "*.png" \
71	       -o -name "*.webp" -o -name "*.webmanifest" \) \
72	    ! -name "sw.js" \
73	    ! -name "sw.js.tmpl" \
74	    ! -name "manifest.webmanifest.tmpl" \
75	    ! -name "og-image.png" \
76	    ! -name "404.html" \
77	    | sed 's|^\./||' | sort
78	)
79	
80	if [[ ${#PRECACHE_REL[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
81	  echo "[build-pwa] no assets found under ${STAGING}" >&2
82	  exit 1
83	fi
84	
85	# Build a JS array literal: ["${BASE}/index.html", "${BASE}/foo-abc.js", …]
86	PRECACHE_JSON="["
87	first=1
88	for rel in "${PRECACHE_REL[@]}"; do
89	  if [[ ${first} -eq 1 ]]; then
90	    first=0
91	  else
92	    PRECACHE_JSON+=","
93	  fi
94	  PRECACHE_JSON+="\"${BASE_PATH}/${rel}\""
95	done
96	PRECACHE_JSON+="]"
97	
98	# Always force "/" (== BASE_PATH/) into the precache so navigation
99	# fallback can find a cached shell at the root URL too.
100	if ! printf '%s\n' "${PRECACHE_REL[@]}" | grep -q '^index\.html$'; then
101	  echo "[build-pwa] warning: index.html not produced by Trunk?" >&2
102	fi
103	
104	# ---- Compute APP_VERSION (content hash) -----------------------------
105	#
106	# Concatenate per-file sha256s, then sha256 the result; keep the first
107	# 12 hex chars. Stable across runs as long as the dist contents are
108	# byte-identical, which is what we want for Cache-Storage versioning.
109	
110	VERSION_RAW="$(
111	  for rel in "${PRECACHE_REL[@]}"; do
112	    shasum -a 256 "${rel}"
113	  done | shasum -a 256 | awk '{print $1}'
114	)"
115	APP_VERSION="${VERSION_RAW:0:12}"
116	
117	cd "${PROJECT_DIR}"
118	
119	# ---- Render templates -----------------------------------------------
120	#
121	# Use `awk` for the substitution because the precache JSON contains
122	# characters (slashes, quotes) that play badly with sed delimiters.
123	
124	render_template() {
125	  local src=$1 dst=$2
126	  awk -v ver="${APP_VERSION}" -v base="${BASE_PATH}" -v list="${PRECACHE_JSON}" '
127	    {
128	      gsub(/__APP_VERSION__/, ver)
129	      gsub(/__BASE_PATH__/, base)
130	      gsub(/__PRECACHE_MANIFEST__/, list)
131	      print
132	    }
133	  ' "${src}" > "${dst}"
134	}
135	
136	render_template "${SW_TMPL}" "${STAGING}/sw.js"
137	render_template "${MF_TMPL}" "${STAGING}/manifest.webmanifest"
138	
139	# Drop the leftover *.tmpl files Trunk copy-file dropped into dist.
140	rm -f "${STAGING}/sw.js.tmpl" "${STAGING}/manifest.webmanifest.tmpl"
141	
142	echo "[build-pwa] version=${APP_VERSION} base='${BASE_PATH}' files=${#PRECACHE_REL[@]}"
143
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 05:48:33Z)**_

我找到兩個問題的明確 root cause：

**Issue 2 (QR Scanner) — CODE BUG**
`clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs:110` 建構 jsQR URL 時用 `{origin}/jsQR.min.js`，但 GitHub Pages 部署在 `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/` 子路徑下，實際載入路徑成了 `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/jsQR.min.js` → 404 → `error` event → "jsQR script load error"。修正：用 `routes::base_path()` 補上子路徑。

**Issue 1 (DataChannel timeout) — 主要是網路 / 路由器問題**
看 `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` 已詳細記錄：你的 SDP 兩邊只有 `.local` mDNS 候選 + 無 STUN，這在某些家用路由器（如 Xiaomi AX9000）會失敗 — 一般 mDNS 服務發現運作，但 WebRTC 動態 `<uuid>.local` resolution 卻不行（原因未完全釐清，但已可重現）。**Approach A (iPhone hotspot) 在這份 pitfall 文檔中已驗證在 1 秒內連得起來**。Code 端能做的只有給更好的診斷 UX，讓你看得到「ICE 卡在 Checking → Disconnected」而不是只看到「timeout，network blocked?」。

在我寫最終 plan 前，想先確認你對 Issue 1 修復範圍的偏好：

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 05:51:39Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**對於 Issue 1（DataChannel 10s timeout），你想修到什麼程度？根本原因是路由器層級的 WebRTC mDNS resolution 失敗（已在 pitfalls 文檔詳記），code 端沒辦法直接修這個。**

Options:
- **診斷 UX 強化（推薦）** - 在 LAN host/join 頁面即時顯示 ICE 狀態（Checking/Failed/Disconnected）、候選數量（mDNS vs srflx），timeout 時帶上最終狀態並建議「試 iPhone hotspot / 勾 STUN」。不改預設、不寫新 server logic。
- **診斷 UX + STUN 預設開啟** - 上面那項加上：把「Use STUN」checkbox 預設改成 ON。注意：根據 pitfall 文檔，STUN srflx 在 Xiaomi AX9000 上實測也沒救到 — 但對其他網路可能有幫助，且不會更糟。
- **只加 console.log，不動 UI** - 最小修改：在 `transport/webrtc.rs` 加 ICE / connection state change 的 console logging，讓你能開 DevTools 看狀態。不改頁面 UX。修最少、view best for self-debug。
- **實作 chess-net signalling endpoint（大改）** - Approach C — 加一個 server-side broker 來轉發 SDP + trickle ICE candidates，繞過 mDNS 問題。可在任何網路 work，但需要 backend、違反「無 server」的初衷。backlog 已記錄為 Phase 5 follow-up。

**Issue 1 還有一個獨立子問題：目前 host 在 `connect_as_host()` 回傳那一刻就 snapshot SDP，但 ICE 仍可能在背景繼續 gather。所以即使 STUN 開了，srflx 候選若晚到也不會出現在 textarea / QR 裡。要修嗎？**

Options:
- **Yes — 點 Copy 時抓最新 localDescription（推薦）** - 把 textarea + QR payload 從 `offer_blob` signal 改成 reactive 讀取 `pc.local_description()`，並監聽 `icegatheringstatechange` 自動 re-render。給 user 多等幾秒能拿到更完整的 SDP。
- **No — 暫不動** - 現在 ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS = 5s 通常已夠 LAN gather 完成；srflx 在 healthy 網路下通常 <1s 到。先看診斷 UX 上線後使用者實際遇到啥再決定。

**Issue 2（jsQR URL 修正）和 Issue 1 修法綁同一 commit 嗎？**

Options:
- **兩個 issue 各自獨立 commit（推薦）** - Issue 2 是純 bug fix（5 行內）、Issue 1 是 diagnostic UX 增強（較大）。分開好 review、好 revert、commit message 也清楚。
- **合併成一個 commit** - 都是 LAN pairing 相關，合在一起也合理。


**Answer:** 診斷 UX 強化（推薦）, Yes — 點 Copy 時抓最新 localDescription（推薦）, 兩個 issue 各自獨立 commit（推薦）
</details></tool-use>

```md
# LAN host/join pairing on GitHub Pages — fix jsQR + ICE diagnostics

## Context

Two bugs reported on `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/lan/host` (LAN WebRTC pairing, Phase 5/5.5):

1. **Copy-text flow reaches "Accept answer" then errors out with**
   `ERROR: DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)`.
   Offer/answer SDPs both have only `.local` mDNS host candidates; toggling
   "Use STUN" doesn't help — error persists, and offer generation visibly slows
   (5 s ICE-gather timeout firing because one of the STUN servers is unreachable).

2. **QR scanner on iOS Safari fails immediately with**
   `JsValue("jsQR script load error")` — the user can't even open the camera flow.

### Root causes

**Issue 2 (clear code bug, the priority fix).**
`clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs:109-110` builds the script URL as:

\```rust
let origin = win.location().origin()?;
let url = format!("{origin}/jsQR.min.js");
\```

On the deployed GitHub Pages target the app lives at `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/...`. `origin` is just `https://daviddwlee84.github.io` (no path), so the constructed URL `{origin}/jsQR.min.js` resolves to a 404 — the asset is actually at `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js`. The script `error` event fires and the loader's promise rejects with `"jsQR script load error"`. The doc comment at lines 12–18 explicitly claims this works on GitHub Pages subpaths, but the implementation doesn't actually consume the base path. The PWA service-worker side handles base paths via `__BASE_PATH__` substitution (`scripts/build-pwa.sh`), and the Leptos router consumes `routes::base_path()` (`src/routes.rs:30`); the jsQR loader just never got plumbed through.

**Issue 1 (not directly fixable in code — it's a network/router-level WebRTC quirk).**
This is the documented pitfall in `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`: modern browsers obfuscate LAN host candidates as `<uuid>.local` mDNS names, and on certain consumer routers (Xiaomi AX9000 confirmed) WebRTC's per-session dynamic `.local` resolution fails even though general mDNS (AirPlay, Mi IoT) works. STUN srflx fallback also doesn't rescue NAT hairpin on the same network (user's STUN-on test confirms this). The known workaround is iPhone-hotspot pairing (Approach A in the pitfall doc); a proper code fix requires a chess-net signalling endpoint (Approach C, backlog `webrtc-lan-pairing.md`).

What we can do in code: make the failure mode *legible* to the user / future debuggers so they don't have to read pitfall docs to know what's happening — surface the live ICE / connection state in the page, log state transitions to the JS console, include the final state in the timeout error, and re-snapshot the SDP at copy time so late-arriving candidates aren't lost.

### Intended outcome

- iOS users can use the QR scanner camera flow on the GH Pages deploy (issue 2 fully fixed).
- When LAN pairing fails (issue 1 — networks where mDNS can't resolve), the user sees what went wrong (e.g. "ICE state: disconnected — try iPhone hotspot or enable STUN") instead of a generic "network blocked?", and the offer/answer text/QR always reflect the latest candidates rather than the half-gathered snapshot. Root-cause network fix (signalling broker) is explicitly deferred to a future Phase 5 follow-up.

---

## Commit 1 — Issue 2: jsQR loader respects base path

**Critical files:**

- `clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs` (lines 98–141, especially 109–110)
- `clients/chess-web/index.html` (comment lines 55–61 — currently lies that the loader works on GH Pages)

**Change:**

In `qr_decode.rs::ensure_loaded()`, replace:

\```rust
let origin = win.location().origin().map_err(|_| JsValue::from_str("no origin"))?;
let url = format!("{origin}/jsQR.min.js");
\```

with:

\```rust
use crate::routes::base_path;

let origin = win.location().origin().map_err(|_| JsValue::from_str("no origin"))?;
let base = base_path();   // "" on server deploy, "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi" on GH Pages
let url = format!("{origin}{base}/jsQR.min.js");
\```

`base_path()` is the existing helper from `routes.rs:30`, baked at build time from `CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH` env var (set by `Makefile` line 72's `build-web-static` recipe). It returns `""` for `make build-web` (root deploy) and `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` for `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi`. The string already has its leading `/` normalized away in the empty case, so concatenation works for both.

Touch up the inaccurate comment block at the top of `ensure_loaded()` (lines 12–18) and the `index.html` comment at lines 55–61 to describe the actual behavior.

---

## Commit 2 — Issue 1: ICE/connection-state diagnostics + re-snapshot SDP on Copy

This is scoped to "make the failure mode visible" — no STUN default change, no signalling-broker work.

**Critical files:**

- `clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs` (install state-change listeners; expose a live `ReadSignal<IceDiag>` on `HostHandshake` / `JoinerHandshake`; widen the per-page SDP exposure to re-read `pc.local_description()` reactively)
- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs` (consume the diag signal in the UI; show inline "ICE: checking → connected" badge; widen the 10-s timeout error to include the final ICE state; re-bind the offer/answer textareas + QR payloads to the live `pc.local_description()` instead of the captured snapshot)
- `clients/chess-web/Cargo.toml` (no changes expected — `RtcIceConnectionState`, `RtcIceGatheringState`, `RtcPeerConnectionState` are all already in the web-sys features at lines 38–45)

### 2a. Wire ICE / connection / gathering state-change handlers

Add to both `connect_as_host()` (around line 302 of `webrtc.rs`, before `create_offer`) and `connect_as_joiner()` (around line 252, after creating the PC):

- `pc.set_oniceconnectionstatechange(...)` — `console.log` the new value of `pc.ice_connection_state()`. Update a reactive `WriteSignal<IceDiag>` field.
- `pc.set_onconnectionstatechange(...)` — same pattern for `pc.connection_state()`.
- `pc.set_onicegatheringstatechange(...)` — keep the existing wait-for-`complete` closure but ALSO push state changes into the diag signal. (Two listeners on the same event is fine; web-sys's setter replaces, so refactor to a single closure that updates the signal AND resolves the gather-complete promise when state == complete.)
- Closures pushed into `_keepalive` (the existing pattern at lines 449–482) so they live as long as the handshake.

Define a small struct in `webrtc.rs`:

\```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct IceDiag {
    pub ice: RtcIceConnectionState,
    pub conn: RtcPeerConnectionState,
    pub gather: RtcIceGatheringState,
}
\```

Add `pub ice_diag: ReadSignal<IceDiag>` to both `HostHandshake` and `JoinerHandshake`. Initialise with the PC's current states at construction time.

### 2b. Use the final ICE state in the 10-s timeout error

In `pages/lan.rs` lines 144–151, change:

\```rust
if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
    set_error_msg.set(Some(
        "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)".into(),
    ));
    ...
}
\```

to read the latest `ice_diag` snapshot and produce a message like:

\```
DataChannel did not open within 10 s — ICE state: <ice>, connection: <conn>.
Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android personal hotspot
(this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution may be failing — see
docs/pwa.md for the documented Xiaomi-router quirk), or enable "Use STUN".
\```

Keep the message ~3 lines, no rant. The state values matter most for debugging.

### 2c. Inline live ICE-state badge on the LAN host/join pages

After the existing status line `<p>"Status: " {move || format!("{:?}", status.get())}</p>` (line 208 of host, line 429 of join), add a second line that displays the current `ice_diag` reactively. Roughly:

\```rust
<Show when=move || handshake_present.get()>
    <p style="font-size:13px;color:#888">
        "ICE: " {move || ice_diag.get().ice_label()}
        " · connection: " {move || ice_diag.get().conn_label()}
        " · gathering: " {move || ice_diag.get().gather_label()}
    </p>
</Show>
\```

Where `*_label()` are small helpers mapping the web-sys enum to short strings ("new", "checking", "connected", "disconnected", "failed", "closed"). This is the load-bearing user-facing change — a user pairing on a flaky network can SEE "ICE: failed" instead of staring at "Status: AcceptingAnswer" for 10 s with no insight.

The `handshake_present` signal is the existing `status.get()` being any state past `Idle`. For host page that's everything after `on_open` succeeds; for joiner that's after `on_generate` succeeds.

### 2d. Re-snapshot SDP on Copy

Currently the offer/answer payload is captured once at `connect_as_*` return time and stored in `offer_blob` / `answer_blob` signals. Background ICE gathering continues; later candidates are silently lost from the user-visible textarea / QR.

Fix: bind the displayed SDP to `pc.local_description()` reactively. The cheapest way:

1. Add a `pub fn current_offer(&self) -> OfferBlob` method on `HostHandshake` that reads the PC's `local_description()` at call time, re-runs `encode_sdp(...)`, and returns a fresh blob. Same `current_answer()` for `JoinerHandshake`.
2. In `lan.rs`, replace the initial `set_offer_blob.set(hh.offer.0.clone())` capture with a derived signal that calls `handshake.borrow().as_ref()?.current_offer()` and reruns on a tick signal driven by `oniceicecandidate` / `onicegatheringstatechange`.
3. Simpler alternative: bind a `create_effect` to `ice_diag` (which already fires on gathering state change) and have it re-run `current_offer()` and set `offer_blob`.

The QR code re-renders automatically because `<QrCodeView payload=Signal::derive(move || offer_blob.get())>` is reactive.

Also wire `pc.set_onicecandidate(...)` to update the diag signal on each candidate arrival — this gives the "Copy offer" button its triggers in real time (each new candidate causes the offer text to refresh). Pure UX: the user can wait, watch the byte count tick up, and copy when they're satisfied.

### 2e. Show candidate counts (optional polish)

In the `ice_diag` struct also include `pub candidates: u32`, incremented by the `onicecandidate` handler. Render as `· candidates: N` in the inline badge. Lets the user see "candidates: 1" (only `.local`, mDNS-only) vs "candidates: 3" (got srflx). Trivial to add since we're already wiring `onicecandidate`.

\```bash
# 1. Workspace sanity (issue 2 + 1 should both compile)

# 2. Native chess-web pure-logic unit tests (15 of them — covers
#    routes.rs::base_path used by qr_decode after the change)
cargo test -p chess-web

# 3. Clippy clean
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings

# 4. WASM build (the actual GH Pages target)
make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi

ls -l clients/chess-web/dist-static/jsQR.min.js
test -f clients/chess-web/dist-static/jsQR.min.js && echo "asset present"

# 6. Local PWA-style serve at the GH Pages subpath
make serve-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi
# → serves on http://localhost:4173/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/

# 7. Use playwright-cli to load the join page (which has the camera
#    button visible), open the scanner, and confirm the loader fires
#    a GET to /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js (not /jsQR.min.js).
#    Specifically:
#    - browser_navigate http://localhost:4173/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/lan/join
#    - browser_evaluate to grant camera mock + flip cam_available
#    - browser_click "📷 Scan offer QR"
#    - browser_network_requests | grep jsQR.min.js
#    - assert path contains /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/
#    - assert response is 200 (not 404)

# 8. Diagnostic UX smoke (issue 1) — Trunk dev server is fine here
make play-web
# Open http://localhost:8080/lan/host in tab A, /lan/join in tab B
# Tab A: tap Open room → confirm "ICE: …" badge appears under Status,
#        confirm console.log output for ice/connection state changes,
#        confirm the byte-count next to Copy offer ticks up as ICE
#        gathers further candidates.
# Tab A: tap Accept answer with a deliberately broken SDP → confirm
#        the 10-s timeout error now includes "ICE state: …"
#        instead of just "network blocked?".

# 9. Full LAN flow on localhost (clean network, no router) — both tabs
#    in same browser actually pair successfully via mDNS bypass through
#    loopback. Acts as regression check that the diagnostics changes
#    didn't break the happy path.
\```

**Cannot verify locally:** the actual user-reported LAN failure (cross-device mDNS resolution failing on their physical router). That requires their two real devices and isn't reproducible from playwright. The diagnostics changes above are intended exactly so the user can self-diagnose the next time the failure recurs, and so the message points them at the documented workaround (iPhone hotspot).

---

## Out of scope (call out in commit body)

- **STUN default = ON.** User's screenshot confirms STUN srflx doesn't bypass the failure on their network; flipping the default would only slow down offer generation (5 s ICE-gather timeout to wait for an unreachable STUN server) without fixing the connection. Keep default OFF.
- **chess-net signalling broker (Approach C).** The right long-term fix for users on networks that fail mDNS *and* STUN-hairpin, but a much bigger change (new server endpoint, persistent ICE-candidate trickle channel, new protocol envelope). Already tracked in `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` as a P3 follow-up.
- **Trickle ICE in copy-paste mode.** Re-snapshotting on Copy is a partial workaround; true trickle requires bidirectional signalling that the manual-copy model can't provide.
- **`getUserMedia` exposes real LAN IPs (Approach C in the pitfall doc).** UX nonstarter — asking for mic permission to start a chess game.

If user wants any of these followups, add to `TODO.md` via `scripts/add-todo.sh` after the two commits land.

# LAN host/join pairing on GitHub Pages — fix jsQR + ICE diagnostics

## Context

Two bugs reported on `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/lan/host` (LAN WebRTC pairing, Phase 5/5.5):

1. **Copy-text flow reaches "Accept answer" then errors out with**
   `ERROR: DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)`.
   Offer/answer SDPs both have only `.local` mDNS host candidates; toggling
   "Use STUN" doesn't help — error persists, and offer generation visibly slows
   (5 s ICE-gather timeout firing because one of the STUN servers is unreachable).

2. **QR scanner on iOS Safari fails immediately with**
   `JsValue("jsQR script load error")` — the user can't even open the camera flow.

### Root causes

**Issue 2 (clear code bug, the priority fix).**
`clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs:109-110` builds the script URL as:

```rust

let url = format!("{origin}/jsQR.min.js");
```

On the deployed GitHub Pages target the app lives at `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/...`. `origin` is just `https://daviddwlee84.github.io` (no path), so the constructed URL `{origin}/jsQR.min.js` resolves to a 404 — the asset is actually at `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js`. The script `error` event fires and the loader's promise rejects with `"jsQR script load error"`. The doc comment at lines 12–18 explicitly claims this works on GitHub Pages subpaths, but the implementation doesn't actually consume the base path. The PWA service-worker side handles base paths via `__BASE_PATH__` substitution (`scripts/build-pwa.sh`), and the Leptos router consumes `routes::base_path()` (`src/routes.rs:30`); the jsQR loader just never got plumbed through.

**Issue 1 (not directly fixable in code — it's a network/router-level WebRTC quirk).**
This is the documented pitfall in `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`: modern browsers obfuscate LAN host candidates as `<uuid>.local` mDNS names, and on certain consumer routers (Xiaomi AX9000 confirmed) WebRTC's per-session dynamic `.local` resolution fails even though general mDNS (AirPlay, Mi IoT) works. STUN srflx fallback also doesn't rescue NAT hairpin on the same network (user's STUN-on test confirms this). The known workaround is iPhone-hotspot pairing (Approach A in the pitfall doc); a proper code fix requires a chess-net signalling endpoint (Approach C, backlog `webrtc-lan-pairing.md`).

What we can do in code: make the failure mode *legible* to the user / future debuggers so they don't have to read pitfall docs to know what's happening — surface the live ICE / connection state in the page, log state transitions to the JS console, include the final state in the timeout error, and re-snapshot the SDP at copy time so late-arriving candidates aren't lost.

### Intended outcome

- iOS users can use the QR scanner camera flow on the GH Pages deploy (issue 2 fully fixed).
- When LAN pairing fails (issue 1 — networks where mDNS can't resolve), the user sees what went wrong (e.g. "ICE state: disconnected — try iPhone hotspot or enable STUN") instead of a generic "network blocked?", and the offer/answer text/QR always reflect the latest candidates rather than the half-gathered snapshot.
