Define a small struct in `webrtc.rs`:

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct IceDiag {
    pub ice: RtcIceConnectionState,
    pub conn: RtcPeerConnectionState,
    pub gather: RtcIceGatheringState,
}
```

Add `pub ice_diag: ReadSignal<IceDiag>` to both `HostHandshake` and `JoinerHandshake`. Initialise with the PC's current states at construction time.

### 2b. Use the final ICE state in the 10-s timeout error

In `pages/lan.rs` lines 144–151, change:

```rust
if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
    set_error_msg.set(Some(
        "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)".into(),
    ));
    ...
}
```

to read the latest `ice_diag` snapshot and produce a message like:

```
DataChannel did not open within 10 s — ICE state: <ice>, connection: <conn>.
Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android personal hotspot
(this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution may be failing — see
docs/pwa.md for the documented Xiaomi-router quirk), or enable "Use STUN".
```

Keep the message ~3 lines, no rant. The state values matter most for debugging.

### 2c. Inline live ICE-state badge on the LAN host/join pages

After the existing status line `<p>"Status: " {move || format!("{:?}", status.get())}</p>` (line 208 of host, line 429 of join), add a second line that displays the current `ice_diag` reactively. Roughly:

```rust
<Show when=move || handshake_present.get()>
    <p style="font-size:13px;color:#888">
        "ICE: " {move || ice_diag.get().ice_label()}
        " · connection: " {move || ice_diag.get().conn_label()}
        " · gathering: " {move || ice_diag.get().gather_label()}
    </p>
</Show>
```

Where `*_label()` are small helpers mapping the web-sys enum to short strings ("new", "checking", "connected", "disconnected", "failed", "closed"). This is the load-bearing user-facing change — a user pairing on a flaky network can SEE "ICE: failed" instead of staring at "Status: AcceptingAnswer" for 10 s with no insight.

The `handshake_present` signal is the existing `status.get()` being any state past `Idle`. For host page that's everything after `on_open` succeeds; for joiner that's after `on_generate` succeeds.

### 2d. Re-snapshot SDP on Copy

Currently the offer/answer payload is captured once at `connect_as_*` return time and stored in `offer_blob` / `answer_blob` signals. Background ICE gathering continues; later candidates are silently lost from the user-visible textarea / QR.

Fix: bind the displayed SDP to `pc.local_description()` reactively. The cheapest way:

1. Add a `pub fn current_offer(&self) -> OfferBlob` method on `HostHandshake` that reads the PC's `local_description()` at call time, re-runs `encode_sdp(...)`, and returns a fresh blob. Same `current_answer()` for `JoinerHandshake`.
2. In `lan.rs`, replace the initial `set_offer_blob.set(hh.offer.0.clone())` capture with a derived signal that calls `handshake.borrow().as_ref()?.current_offer()` and reruns on a tick signal driven by `oniceicecandidate` / `onicegatheringstatechange`.
3. Simpler alternative: bind a `create_effect` to `ice_diag` (which already fires on gathering state change) and have it re-run `current_offer()` and set `offer_blob`.

The QR code re-renders automatically because `<QrCodeView payload=Signal::derive(move || offer_blob.get())>` is reactive.

Also wire `pc.set_onicecandidate(...)` to update the diag signal on each candidate arrival — this gives the "Copy offer" button its triggers in real time (each new candidate causes the offer text to refresh). Pure UX: the user can wait, watch the byte count tick up, and copy when they're satisfied.

### 2e. Show candidate counts (optional polish)

In the `ice_diag` struct also include `pub candidates: u32`, incremented by the `onicecandidate` handler. Render as `· candidates: N` in the inline badge. Lets the user see "candidates: 1" (only `.local`, mDNS-only) vs "candidates: 3" (got srflx). Trivial to add since we're already wiring `onicecandidate`.

---

**Cannot verify locally:** the actual user-reported LAN failure (cross-device mDNS resolution failing on their physical router). That requires their two real devices and isn't reproducible from playwright. The diagnostics changes above are intended exactly so the user can self-diagnose the next time the failure recurs, and so the message points them at the documented workaround (iPhone hotspot).

---

## Out of scope (call out in commit body)

- **STUN default = ON.** User's screenshot confirms STUN srflx doesn't bypass the failure on their network; flipping the default would only slow down offer generation (5 s ICE-gather timeout to wait for an unreachable STUN server) without fixing the connection. Keep default OFF.
- **chess-net signalling broker (Approach C).** The right long-term fix for users on networks that fail mDNS *and* STUN-hairpin, but a much bigger change (new server endpoint, persistent ICE-candidate trickle channel, new protocol envelope). Already tracked in `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` as a P3 follow-up.
- **Trickle ICE in copy-paste mode.** Re-snapshotting on Copy is a partial workaround; true trickle requires bidirectional signalling that the manual-copy model can't provide.
- **`getUserMedia` exposes real LAN IPs (Approach C in the pitfall doc).** UX nonstarter — asking for mic permission to start a chess game.

If user wants any of these followups, add to `TODO.md` via `scripts/add-todo.sh` after the two commits land.

```text
fn main() {
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=CHESS_WEB_HOSTING");
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH");

    let hosting = std::env::var("CHESS_WEB_HOSTING").unwrap_or_else(|_| "server".to_string());
    let hosting = match hosting.as_str() {
        "static" => "static",
        _ => "server",
    };

    let raw_base = std::env::var("CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH").unwrap_or_default();
    let base = normalize_base_path(&raw_base);

    println!("cargo:rustc-env=CHESS_WEB_HOSTING={hosting}");
    println!("cargo:rustc-env=CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH={base}");
}

fn normalize_base_path(raw: &str) -> String {
    let trimmed = raw.trim().trim_matches('/');
    if trimmed.is_empty() {
        String::new()
    } else {
        format!("/{trimmed}")
    }
}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
444	
445	/// Block until `pc.iceGatheringState == "complete"`, OR
446	/// [`ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS`] elapses (whichever comes first).
447	async fn wait_for_ice_complete(
448	    pc: &RtcPeerConnection,
449	    keepalive: &mut Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>,
450	) {
451	    if pc.ice_gathering_state() == RtcIceGatheringState::Complete {
452	        return;
453	    }
454	    let promise = js_sys::Promise::new(&mut |resolve, _reject| {
455	        let pc_inner = pc.clone();
456	        let resolve_for_state = resolve.clone();
457	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
458	            if pc_inner.ice_gathering_state() == RtcIceGatheringState::Complete {
459	                let _ = resolve_for_state.call0(&JsValue::NULL);
460	            }
461	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
462	        pc.set_onicegatheringstatechange(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
463	        keepalive.push(cb);
464	
465	        // Hard-cap the wait. Late `complete` events still fire but
466	        // promise resolution is idempotent.
467	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
468	            let resolve_for_timeout = resolve.clone();
469	            let pc_for_timeout = pc.clone();
470	            let timeout_cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
471	                web_sys::console::warn_2(
472	                    &"[webrtc] ICE gather timeout — proceeding with whatever candidates we have. Final state:".into(),
473	                    &format!("{:?}", pc_for_timeout.ice_gathering_state()).into(),
474	                );
475	                let _ = resolve_for_timeout.call0(&JsValue::NULL);
476	            }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
477	            let _ = win.set_timeout_with_callback_and_timeout_and_arguments_0(
478	                timeout_cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref(),
479	                ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS,
480	            );
481	            keepalive.push(timeout_cb);
482	        }
483	    });
484	    let _ = JsFuture::from(promise).await;
485	}
```

```text
41	use js_sys::{Array, Reflect};
42	use leptos::*;
43	use wasm_bindgen::closure::Closure;
44	use wasm_bindgen::{JsCast, JsValue};
45	use wasm_bindgen_futures::JsFuture;
46	use web_sys::{
47	    MessageEvent, RtcConfiguration, RtcDataChannel, RtcDataChannelEvent, RtcDataChannelInit,
48	    RtcDataChannelState, RtcIceGatheringState, RtcPeerConnection, RtcSdpType,
49	    RtcSessionDescriptionInit, RtcSignalingState,
50	};
51	
52	use super::{ConnState, Incoming, Session, Transport};
53	// SECTION: public types (IceMode, WebRtcConfig, OfferBlob, AnswerBlob)
54	
55	/// `RtcPeerConnection` ICE configuration.
56	///
57	/// `LanOnly` is the production default — `iceServers: []`, pure mDNS
58	/// `<uuid>.local` host candidates only. No external dependencies.
59	///
60	/// `WithStun` adds a multi-server STUN list for the `srflx` candidates.
61	/// Useful as a fallback when LAN P2P fails (e.g. router blocks WebRTC's
62	/// `<uuid>.local` resolution; see
63	/// `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`). Server list ordered for
64	/// CN reachability — `stun.miwifi.com` and `stun.qq.com` first, Google
65	/// last (Google STUN is blocked by the GFW from mainland China).
66	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
67	pub enum IceMode {
68	    LanOnly,
69	    WithStun,
70	}
71	
72	/// Knobs for opening a WebRTC session. Defaults to `LanOnly` — the
73	/// "no external dependency" production case.
74	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]
75	pub struct WebRtcConfig {
76	    pub ice_mode: IceMode,
77	}
78	
79	impl Default for WebRtcConfig {
80	    fn default() -> Self {
81	        Self { ice_mode: IceMode::LanOnly }
82	    }
83	}
84	
85	/// Maximum time we wait for `iceGatheringState == "complete"` before
86	/// giving up and producing SDP with whatever candidates have arrived.
87	///
88	/// Caps the failure mode where one of the configured STUN servers is
89	/// unreachable (e.g. `stun.l.google.com` from mainland China — blocked
90	/// by the GFW), which otherwise hangs the whole `connect_as_*` flow
91	/// indefinitely.
92	///
93	/// 5s is generous: pure-LAN gathering completes in 100-200 ms, healthy
94	/// STUN gathering in <1 s. If we hit the timeout we still produce a
95	/// valid SDP — there will just be fewer candidates than "ideal".
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
383	    spawn_local(async move {
384	        if has_camera().await {
385	            set_cam_available.set(true);
386	        }
387	    });
388	
389	    // Joiner side: store the JoinerHandshake so we can pull its
390	    // session out once the DC opens.
391	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<JoinerHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
392	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
393	
394	    // Holder pattern: spawn_local runs without a Leptos owner context,
395	    // so a `create_effect` inside it would be GC'd immediately (no
396	    // subscriptions retained — `state.set(Open)` would fire but no
397	    // effect would react). Solution: define the holder signals and
398	    // the effect at component scope (properly owned), and have
399	    // spawn_local just `set` the holders. The component-scope effect
400	    // re-runs when the holders change AND when the inner state
401	    // signal changes.
402	    let (joiner_state_holder, set_joiner_state_holder) =
403	        create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<ConnState>>>(None);
404	    let session_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<Session>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
405	
406	    {
407	        let session_holder = session_holder.clone();
408	        create_effect(move |_| {
409	            // Read both signals to register subscriptions:
410	            //   * joiner_state_holder fires once when handshake completes.
411	            //   * the inner state signal fires when DC opens.
412	            if let Some(state_sig) = joiner_state_holder.get() {
413	                if state_sig.get() == ConnState::Open {
414	                    if let Some(session) = session_holder.borrow().clone() {
415	                        set_play_session.set(Some(session));
416	                        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Playing);
417	                    }
418	                }
419	            }
420	        });
421	    }
422	
423	    let handshake_for_gen = handshake.clone();
424	    let session_holder_for_gen = session_holder.clone();
425	    let on_generate: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
426	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), JoinStatus::Idle) {
427	            return;
428	        }
429	        let blob = offer_input.get_untracked();
430	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
431	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the host's offer SDP first".into()));
432	            return;
433	        }
434	        set_error_msg.set(None);
435	        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Generating);
436	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
437	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
438	        };
439	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_gen.clone();
440	        let session_holder = session_holder_for_gen.clone();
441	        spawn_local(async move {
442	            match connect_as_joiner(cfg, OfferBlob(blob)).await {
443	                Ok(jh) => {
444	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.answer.0.clone());
445	                    let state = jh.session.state;
446	                    *session_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(jh.session.clone());
447	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(jh);
448	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::WaitingForOpen);
449	                    // Triggers the component-scope effect to subscribe
450	                    // to the state signal. Once DC opens, that effect
451	                    // flips status → Playing.
452	                    set_joiner_state_holder.set(Some(state));
453	                }
454	                Err(e) => {
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Tiny SPA-aware static server for local PWA verification.

GitHub Pages serves the dist with a `404.html` fallback that's a copy of
`index.html`, so direct links to client-side routes like
`/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/local/xiangqi` work. Plain `python3 -m
http.server` does not — it returns its own error page on a missing
file, which is why `make serve-web-static` was 404'ing on every SPA
route.

This handler mirrors the GH Pages behaviour: any GET inside the base
scope that doesn't resolve to a file falls back to `<base>/index.html`.
Outside the scope (or for paths with extensions like .js / .wasm),
return the normal 404 so a real missing asset is still surfaced.

Usage:
    serve-spa.py <serve-dir> <port> <base-prefix>
    # e.g. serve-spa.py /tmp/staging 4173 /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/
    # base-prefix may be "/" or empty for root-served deployments.

No external deps — stdlib only, runs on the bundled python3 of any
modern macOS / Linux dev box.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, SimpleHTTPRequestHandler

if len(sys.argv) != 4:
    print(__doc__, file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(2)

SERVE_DIR = os.path.abspath(sys.argv[1])
PORT = int(sys.argv[2])
BASE = "/" + sys.argv[3].strip("/")  # always leading slash, no trailing
if BASE == "/":
    BASE = ""  # treat as root-served

# A path is "asset-like" if it has a file extension we precache or
```

# Browser Automation with playwright-cli

## Quick start

```bash
# open new browser
playwright-cli open
# navigate to a page
playwright-cli goto https://playwright.dev
# interact with the page using refs from the snapshot
playwright-cli click e15
playwright-cli type "page.click"
playwright-cli press Enter
# take a screenshot (rarely used, as snapshot is more common)
playwright-cli screenshot
# close the browser
playwright-cli close
```

## Commands

### Core

```bash
playwright-cli open
# open and navigate right away
playwright-cli open https://example.com/
playwright-cli goto https://playwright.dev
playwright-cli type "search query"
playwright-cli click e3
playwright-cli dblclick e7
# --submit presses Enter after filling the element
playwright-cli fill e5 "user@example.com"  --submit
playwright-cli drag e2 e8
# drop files or data onto an element (from outside the page)
playwright-cli drop e4 --path=./image.png
playwright-cli drop e4 --data="text/plain=hello world"
playwright-cli hover e4
playwright-cli select e9 "option-value"
playwright-cli upload ./document.pdf
playwright-cli check e12
playwright-cli uncheck e12
playwright-cli snapshot
playwright-cli eval "document.title"
playwright-cli eval "el => el.textContent" e5
# get element id, class, or any attribute not visible in the snapshot
playwright-cli eval "el => el.id" e5
playwright-cli eval "el => el.getAttribute('data-testid')" e5
playwright-cli dialog-accept
playwright-cli dialog-accept "confirmation text"
playwright-cli dialog-dismiss
playwright-cli resize 1920 1080
playwright-cli close
```

### Navigation

```bash
playwright-cli go-back
playwright-cli go-forward
playwright-cli reload
```

### Keyboard

```bash
playwright-cli press Enter
playwright-cli press ArrowDown
playwright-cli keydown Shift
playwright-cli keyup Shift
```

### Mouse

```bash
playwright-cli mousemove 150 300
playwright-cli mousedown
playwright-cli mousedown right
playwright-cli mouseup
playwright-cli mouseup right
playwright-cli mousewheel 0 100
```

### Save as

```bash
playwright-cli screenshot
playwright-cli screenshot e5
playwright-cli screenshot --filename=page.png
playwright-cli pdf --filename=page.pdf
```

### Tabs

```bash
playwright-cli tab-list
playwright-cli tab-new
playwright-cli tab-new https://example.com/page
playwright-cli tab-close
playwright-cli tab-close 2
playwright-cli tab-select 0
```

### Storage

```bash
playwright-cli state-save
playwright-cli state-save auth.json
playwright-cli state-load auth.json

# Cookies
playwright-cli cookie-list
playwright-cli cookie-list --domain=example.com
playwright-cli cookie-get session_id
playwright-cli cookie-set session_id abc123
playwright-cli cookie-set session_id abc123 --domain=example.com --httpOnly --secure
playwright-cli cookie-delete session_id
playwright-cli cookie-clear

# LocalStorage
playwright-cli localstorage-list
playwright-cli localstorage-get theme
playwright-cli localstorage-set theme dark
playwright-cli localstorage-delete theme
playwright-cli localstorage-clear

# SessionStorage
playwright-cli sessionstorage-list
playwright-cli sessionstorage-get step
playwright-cli sessionstorage-set step 3
playwright-cli sessionstorage-delete step
playwright-cli sessionstorage-clear
```

### Network

```bash
playwright-cli route "**/*.jpg" --status=404
playwright-cli route "https://api.example.com/**" --body='{"mock": true}'
playwright-cli route-list
playwright-cli unroute "**/*.jpg"
playwright-cli unroute
```

### DevTools

```bash
playwright-cli console
playwright-cli console warning
playwright-cli requests
playwright-cli request 5
playwright-cli run-code "async page => await page.context().grantPermissions(['geolocation'])"
playwright-cli run-code --filename=script.js
playwright-cli tracing-start
playwright-cli tracing-stop
playwright-cli video-start video.webm
playwright-cli video-chapter "Chapter Title" --description="Details" --duration=2000
playwright-cli video-stop

# launch the dashboard for UI review / design feedback — user annotates the page, you receive the annotated screenshot, snapshot, and notes
playwright-cli show --annotate

# generate a Playwright locator for an element from its ref or selector
playwright-cli generate-locator e5 --raw

# show a persistent highlight overlay for an element, optionally with a custom style
playwright-cli highlight e5
playwright-cli highlight e5 --style="outline: 3px dashed red"
# hide a single element highlight, or all page highlights when no target is given
playwright-cli highlight e5 --hide
playwright-cli highlight --hide
```

## Raw output

The global `--raw` option strips page status, generated code, and snapshot sections from the output, returning only the result value. Use it to pipe command output into other tools. Commands that don't produce output return nothing.

```bash
playwright-cli --raw eval "JSON.stringify(performance.timing)" | jq '.loadEventEnd - .navigationStart'
playwright-cli --raw eval "JSON.stringify([...document.querySelectorAll('a')].map(a => a.href))" > links.json
playwright-cli --raw snapshot > before.yml
playwright-cli click e5
playwright-cli --raw snapshot > after.yml
diff before.yml after.yml
TOKEN=$(playwright-cli --raw cookie-get session_id)
playwright-cli --raw localstorage-get theme
```

For structured output wrapping every reply as JSON, pass --json
```bash
playwright-cli list --json
```

## Open parameters
```bash
# Use specific browser when creating session
playwright-cli open --browser=chrome
playwright-cli open --browser=firefox
playwright-cli open --browser=webkit
playwright-cli open --browser=msedge

# Use persistent profile (by default profile is in-memory)
playwright-cli open --persistent
# Use persistent profile with custom directory
playwright-cli open --profile=/path/to/profile

# Connect to browser via Playwright Extension
playwright-cli attach --extension=chrome

# Connect to a running Chrome or Edge by channel name
playwright-cli attach --cdp=chrome
playwright-cli attach --cdp=msedge

# Connect to a running browser via CDP endpoint
playwright-cli attach --cdp=http://localhost:9222

# Start with config file
playwright-cli open --config=my-config.json

# Close the browser
playwright-cli close
# Detach from an attached browser (leaves the external browser running)
playwright-cli -s=msedge detach
# Delete user data for the default session
playwright-cli delete-data
```

## Snapshots

After each command, playwright-cli provides a snapshot of the current browser state.

```bash
> playwright-cli goto https://example.com
### Page
- Page URL: https://example.com/
- Page Title: Example Domain
### Snapshot
[Snapshot](.playwright-cli/page-2026-02-14T19-22-42-679Z.yml)
```

You can also take a snapshot on demand using `playwright-cli snapshot` command. All the options below can be combined as needed.

```bash
# default - save to a file with timestamp-based name
playwright-cli snapshot

# save to file, use when snapshot is a part of the workflow result
playwright-cli snapshot --filename=after-click.yaml

# snapshot an element instead of the whole page
playwright-cli snapshot "#main"

# limit snapshot depth for efficiency, take a partial snapshot afterwards
playwright-cli snapshot --depth=4
playwright-cli snapshot e34

# include each element's bounding box as [box=x,y,width,height]
playwright-cli snapshot --boxes
```

## Targeting elements

By default, use refs from the snapshot to interact with page elements.

```bash
# get snapshot with refs
playwright-cli snapshot

# interact using a ref
playwright-cli click e15
```

You can also use css selectors or Playwright locators.

```bash
# css selector
playwright-cli click "#main > button.submit"

# role locator
playwright-cli click "getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' })"

# test id
playwright-cli click "getByTestId('submit-button')"
```

## Browser Sessions

```bash
# create new browser session named "mysession" with persistent profile
playwright-cli -s=mysession open example.com --persistent
# same with manually specified profile directory (use when requested explicitly)
playwright-cli -s=mysession open example.com --profile=/path/to/profile
playwright-cli -s=mysession click e6
playwright-cli -s=mysession close  # stop a named browser
playwright-cli -s=mysession delete-data  # delete user data for persistent session

playwright-cli list
# Close all browsers
playwright-cli close-all
# Forcefully kill all browser processes
playwright-cli kill-all
```

## Installation

If global `playwright-cli` command is not available, try a local version via `npx playwright-cli`:

```bash
npx --no-install playwright-cli --version
```

When local version is available, use `npx playwright-cli` in all commands. Otherwise, install `playwright-cli` as a global command:

```bash
npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest
```

## Example: Form submission

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com/form
playwright-cli snapshot

playwright-cli fill e1 "user@example.com"
playwright-cli fill e2 "password123"
playwright-cli click e3
playwright-cli snapshot
playwright-cli close
```

## Example: Multi-tab workflow

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli tab-new https://example.com/other
playwright-cli tab-list
playwright-cli tab-select 0
playwright-cli snapshot
playwright-cli close
```

## Example: Debugging with DevTools

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli click e4
playwright-cli fill e7 "test"
playwright-cli console
playwright-cli requests
playwright-cli close
```

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli tracing-start
playwright-cli click e4
playwright-cli fill e7 "test"
playwright-cli tracing-stop
playwright-cli close
```

## Example: Interactive session

Ask the user for UI review or design feedback. The user draws boxes on the live page and types comments; you receive the annotated screenshot, the snapshot of the marked region, and the user's notes. Use this whenever the user asks for "UI review", "design feedback", or to "ask the user what they think / want / mean":

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli show --annotate
```

### Commit 1 — jsQR URL fix

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:18:28Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>


_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:18:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit 1: jsQR URL fix

```bash
git add clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs clients/chess-web/index.html && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chess-web): jsQR loader respects build-time base_path so GH Pages subpath deploys can load the camera scanner

`qr_decode::ensure_loaded` was building the script URL as
`{origin}/jsQR.min.js`, which 404'd on the GitHub Pages deploy at
`/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/` because `origin` doesn't include the subpath
(`window.location.origin` is just `https://daviddwlee84.github.io`).
The asset itself was correctly emitted at `{base}/jsQR.min.js` and the
PWA service worker precached it under the same path; only the runtime
loader missed the prefix.

Reported symptom on iOS Safari at /lan/join (Phase 5.5 LAN pairing):
"QR scanner library failed to load: JsValue(\"jsQR script load error\")".

Fix: read `crate::routes::base_path()` (baked at build time from
`CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH`, empty for server-root deploys, `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi`
for GH Pages) and concatenate it into the URL. Tighten the misleading
doc comments in qr_decode.rs and index.html.

Verified: rebuilt with `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi`
and confirmed via playwright that the page's runtime context can fetch
the new URL pattern (`/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js` → 200) while
the old broken pattern (`/jsQR.min.js`) still 404s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

```text
[main 7220e90] fix(chess-web): jsQR loader respects build-time base_path so GH Pages subpath deploys can load the camera scanner
 2 files changed, 23 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
```
</details></tool-use>

```bash
git add clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs .claude/plans/https-daviddwlee84-github-io-chinese-ch-imperative-sun.md .specstory/history/2026-05-14_05-43-00Z-https-daviddwlee84-github-io.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(chess-web): live ICE diagnostics on /lan/host and /lan/join — surface the real failure mode when pairing times out

The reported "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed
(network blocked?)" error fired on the production GH Pages deploy with
the SDP showing only `.local` mDNS host candidates. The actual root
cause is the documented router-level WebRTC mDNS quirk in
pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md — not anything we can fix
in code. But the existing UX gave the user zero insight into WHY the
DC didn't open; even toggling "Use STUN" produced the same opaque
timeout (because STUN srflx doesn't bypass the failure on the same
network either).

Changes — make the failure mode legible:

* `IceDiag` struct (live `ice` / `conn` / `gather` states + candidate
  count) exposed as a `ReadSignal` on `HostHandshake` /
  `JoinerHandshake`. Updated by listeners installed in
  `install_ice_diag_handlers` (oniceconnectionstatechange,
  onconnectionstatechange, onicecandidate) plus the existing
  `wait_for_ice_complete` for the gathering state. Every transition is
  also `console.log`'d so DevTools shows the full progression.
* `pages/lan.rs` renders the diag as an inline badge below the existing
  "Status: …" line on both host and join pages — user sees e.g. "ICE:
  failed · connection: failed · gathering: complete · candidates: 1"
  instead of staring at "AcceptingAnswer" for 10 s.
* The 10s timeout error now includes the final `IceDiag` line and
  points at the iPhone-hotspot / Use-STUN workarounds.
* `current_offer()` / `current_answer()` re-encode the PC's
  `local_description()` at call time, and an effect in lan.rs re-runs
  them on every diag change. Late-arriving srflx candidates (when STUN
  is enabled but the initial 5s gather timeout fired) now appear in the
  textarea / QR rather than being silently dropped from the snapshot
  captured at handshake-return time.

Out of scope (explicit decisions in the plan):
* STUN default stays OFF — user's own test confirmed default-on STUN
  doesn't help on the affected network and slows offer generation.
* No chess-net signalling broker (Approach C from
  backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md). That's a separate Phase 5 follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:19:25Z)**_

Both commits landed cleanly. Quick summary of what shipped:

**Commit 1 `7220e90`** — `fix(chess-web): jsQR loader respects build-time base_path…`
- `qr_decode.rs::ensure_loaded` now builds `{origin}{routes::base_path()}/jsQR.min.js`. On GH Pages that resolves to `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js` (200) instead of the broken root path (404).
- Verified end-to-end with `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` + a playwright in-page `fetch()` against both the new and old URL patterns.

**Commit 2 `38736f6`** — `feat(chess-web): live ICE diagnostics on /lan/host and /lan/join…`
- New `IceDiag` (`ice`/`conn`/`gather`/`candidates`) on both handshakes, fed by `oniceconnectionstatechange` + `onconnectionstatechange` + `onicecandidate` + the existing gather-state listener — also `console.log`'d for DevTools.
- Inline "ICE: … · connection: … · gathering: … · candidates: N" badge under Status on both pages.
- 10s timeout error now reads e.g. `… ICE: failed · connection: failed · candidates: 1. Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android personal hotspot (this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution may be failing), or enable "Use STUN".`
- `current_offer()` / `current_answer()` + reactive effects so the textarea/QR refresh on each new candidate — late srflx arrivals no longer get dropped on the floor.

The actual router-level mDNS failure isn't fixable in code (documented in `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`); next time it bites, the badge will name the exact stuck state and point at the iPhone-hotspot workaround. The chess-net signalling-broker route is still tracked in `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` if you want a network-agnostic fix later.

Workspace is clean (`cargo check`/`clippy`/`test -p chess-web`/`build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-web` all green; 86 chess-web tests pass).
